instructions: Refactor the `module_scope` field in the Pydantic models (e.g., `AgentDirective` in jitsu.models) to be a `list[str]` instead of a single `str`. Update the boundary security checks in `jitsu.core` (likely inside the Executor or Orchestrator) to validate file edits against a list of allowed prefixes instead of a single string. Update `src/jitsu/prompts.py` so the Planner is instructed to generate a list of strings for `module_scope`. Fix all corresponding tests to ensure 100% test coverage and no linting bypasses.
anti_patterns: Breaking existing string-based module_scope usages without migrating them in the tests.
Using `# type: ignore` to bypass Pydantic schema typing errors.
completion_criteria: module_scope is now strongly typed as list[str] in Pydantic models.
Executor boundary logic validates file edits against multiple prefix boundaries.
prompts.py instructs Planner to return a list of strings.
just verify passes cleanly with 100% coverage.

instructions: Extract hardcoded LLM model names out of the source code and into a centralized configuration. 1. Create or update a config module (e.g., `src/jitsu/config.py`) using `pydantic-settings` to manage default model names (e.g., `default_planner_model`, `default_executor_model`). 2. Update `src/jitsu/cli.py` and the core execution engine to read these models from the config instead of hardcoding strings. 3. Ensure the CLI `--model` flag properly overrides the config. 4. Update tests to assert that models can be dynamically configured. Ensure 100% test coverage and no linting bypasses.
anti_patterns: Leaving hardcoded provider/model strings in the core execution logic.
Failing to cover the new configuration loading logic with tests.
completion_criteria: Model names are loaded via a central configuration system/Pydantic BaseSettings.
CLI and Core modules no longer contain hardcoded model strings.
just verify passes cleanly with 100% test coverage.
